title: Leaf-Shaped Nesting Tables
summary: A set of three graduated leaf-shaped side tables cut from oak plywood on purchased furniture legs.
description:
Nesting tables usually come as a matched rectangular set; these three lean into an organic leaf shape instead, which reads as sculptural rather than purely functional while still stacking together the same way a traditional nesting set does.

Each top is cut from its own **hand-drawn paper template**, sized to graduate from largest to smallest so the set nests cleanly — there's no fixed numeric cut list here on purpose, since the shape and scale are meant to fit your own space rather than a standard dimension.

**Purchased furniture legs**, not shop-built ones, are the right call here — a turned or tapered leg that matches the tops' organic shape is a specialized turning job, and buying legs lets the build focus on the tabletop shaping instead.
timeLabel: 2.5–4 hrs for the set of three

tools.note: Trues up long straight edges after the curved cuts.

steps.title: Make the templates
steps.body: Cut craft paper templates in a leaf shape, in three graduated sizes sized to your own space and to nest together.

steps.title: Trace and cut the tops
steps.body: Trace the templates onto the oak plywood, orienting the grain to follow the leaf shape, then cut out each tabletop with a jigsaw and true up any long straight edges with a circular saw.

steps.title: Band the edges
steps.body: Apply edge banding to all cut edges and trim flush.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the purchased furniture legs to the underside of each top with pocket screws and glue.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand and finish all three tables.
